evidence.title: White House release framing Maduro event as capture and extradition
evidence.source_description: Official White House release retrieved with web.open on 2026-06-30; excerpted to preserve the relevant official characterization and adverse public-reaction context.
evidence.relevance: Official White House source characterizing the Maduro event as capture and extradition, with Maduro detained on U.S. soil to face justice. Supports the defense distinction between a capture/law-enforcement action and territorial occupation or operational control of a Venezuelan population center.

evidence.title: White House fact sheet on Venezuelan oil revenue after operation
evidence.source_description: Official White House fact sheet, retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official White House post-operation source confirming U.S. armed forces conducted an extraordinary military operation in support of law enforcement to bring Maduro to justice and linking U.S. actions to Venezuelan stability and oil revenue control.

evidence.title: UN Security Council record S/PV.10085 on Venezuela
evidence.source_description: United Nations Digital Library PDF, Security Council provisional verbatim record S/PV.10085, retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Secondary/fallback Security Council record for the Jan. 5, 2026 emergency meeting on Venezuela, reflecting international treatment of the U.S. action and preserving context if official U.S. sources are argued to conflict.

evidence.title: War.gov article: Trump Announces U.S. Military's Capture of Maduro
evidence.source_description: Official U.S. Department of War / Defense Department News webpage, retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Primary official Defense/War Department source confirming a Jan. 3, 2026 U.S. military operation in Caracas, Venezuela, with a ground-based extraction force, special operations forces, and statements about Maduro being out of power and U.S. oversight of Venezuela.

evidence.title: White House release: Rubio on Venezuela operation
evidence.source_description: Official White House release, retrieved by web.open on 2026-06-30.
evidence.relevance: Official White House source confirming U.S. forces were on the ground in Venezuela for about two hours to capture Maduro, while also providing limiting/adverse language that it was not a war, invasion, or extended military operation.
